title: Whos worse Gendo or the teacher
display_title: Whos worse Gendo or the teacher
wikitext:
[]: Who's worse? Gendo or the teacher?             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Thu Mar 14, 2013 3:56 am Whose worse? Gendo, a father who humanly abandons Shinji to someone else or the Teacher who lets Shinji be the way he is with no friends and not much support to the point where he isn't even mentioned ever. As much as we like to make Gendo out to be the bad guy, he isn't all "Screw you Shinji I'm going to abandon you cause I'm totally evil Mwhaha". He is a cold and manipulative, that's a true. However, he was afraid of people, Shinji included. Yui help or tried to help change that. When she died, he ran back in that shell and gotten worse effecting Shinji as well as the Akagis. However, he did try to give Shinji a good home. Now from what I can tell, this Sensei, we hear little to nothing of him/her. I think one part of dialogue cut from EoE implied that he/she was a real teacher rather than an authority figure. Its also implied that its someone specifically Shinji lives with rather than living in an orphanage. Point is that its personal. However, look how Shinji turned out. He has no friends, hes scared of making some because of his abandonment issues. He has low self esteem to the extent where he feels that he has to pilot an Eva risking his health and life to find meaning. We hear nothing of the teacher. Dose Shinji hate him or even like him? Usually, when things like this happen, they are usually people who hate Gendo and try to be a good guardian. The Teacher, neglects him even worse. Its not like Shinji is a bad kid really. In the Manga, Gendo is much more closer to the "Mwhaha I'm evil interpretation" where he didn't do it out of fear rather than hate. He doesn't even praise him and calls him useless in a tone that just sounds a lot more brutal. He leaves him to Yui's brother instead. Shinji in the Manga is more prone to anger but isn't a complete trouble maker blowing up animals or crap like that. He did hear in earshot that they didn't even want him when he got on one of his fits. They did try to do good things for him like give him his own shed but that isn't saying much. The Manga did note one thing and I think this applies to the anime as well. When Shinji started piloting, he didn't get a call from his relatives at all. In episode 22, Shinji even mentions that he didn't even get calls. So who is worse? The father that abandons him or the guardian that neglects him?

[]:             Quote Post by Felipe » Thu Mar 14, 2013 10:54 am Do I have to be the one to do this? I am an English teacher, but come on, I don't start until 6.

[]:             Quote Post by Lelouch34 » Thu Mar 14, 2013 2:16 pm The Teacher we don't really know enough to assign blame, but the teacher might deserve blame. Gendo however is the one who left Shinji alone at a station, and only called him when he had a need for Shinji. All because Gendo is manipulative and doesn't have a spine to care for his kid to channel his grief in an effective manner. Also, don't blame Shinji for being upset after his mother died. He is a kid that need's comfort, and support which he didn't recieve in particular from the person as a child he looked up to Gendo. No one can ever replace your real father in you life for Shinji there is no exception to this rule. Also, I don't blame Yui because she gave up her life so Shinji had a future, and was still there in the Eva for Shinji. Also, she did try to make Gendo more caring for his, and Shinji's sake. The teacher being uncaring would make it worse. However Shinji never syas anything bad about the teacher, but does about Gendo. Therefore It all goes back to Gendo because he is not being the father as he should be instead he tries to pass the role off to someone else instead of caring for Shinji like any dad should. Also, if Gendo put Shinji in a bad environment than Gendo deserve's even more blame, Father is a title given by birth, but you have to work to earn the title Dad from your child.

[]: Re: Who's worse? Gendo or the teacher?             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Mar 14, 2013 5:47 pm SimplyMason0 wrote: Whose worse? Gendo, a father who humanly abandons Shinji to someone else or the Teacher who lets Shinji be the way he is with no friends and not much support to the point where he isn't even mentioned ever.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Mar 14, 2013 10:00 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Let's take a step back here.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Mar 15, 2013 3:04 am BC Baron wrote: Right from the beginning of the series we see Shinji show up in the middle of a warzone without his guardian accompanying him.

[]: Re: Who's worse? Gendo or the teacher?             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Fri Mar 15, 2013 9:13 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Given these references I'd like to deal with some of the main points here. Almost all, of the problems with Shinji can be laid at Gendo's feet. We don't need to involve his Sensei, nor do we have any hard evidence for doing so. We have no proof of mistreatment or neglect. If anything, I think that this Sensei was more likely to have been a source of stability in Shinji's life. As to Shinji having no friends, again we have no real evidence. We do know that he makes friends in Tokyo-3 -- Toji, Kensuke, and Hikari to a lesser degree. He even makes considerable effort to befriend an ever ambivalent Rei, and also has a common "feud" relationship with Asuka. Is it likely he only learned how to make friends on coming to Tokyo-3? And on the matter of Shinji's overall adjustment, while it is true that he has deep seated issues -- principally surrounding Gendo -- and while he eventually does become a wreck (under mitigating circumstances), we don't know if it is true to say that Shinji was always a total wreck. We are shown glimpses of a happier child, dare I say a more adjusted one Should we not lay the "blame" for these images at his Sensei's feet as well? I don't think it's so easy to come to a quick condemnation here.

[]: Re: Who's worse? Gendo or the teacher?             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Mar 15, 2013 12:32 pm I'm in the same boat as OMF on this one. SimplyMason0 wrote: So why shouldn't he be more adjusted and normal?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Mar 15, 2013 3:14 pm If you're asking who is the worse person then it's Gendo by far. If you're asking who is responsible for Shinji ending up the way he did then they both have a degree of blame (along with a few other people) but Gendo is the ghost that constantly haunts Shinji. That should make it clear who had the bigger impact on him.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Mar 15, 2013 6:41 pm BC Baron wrote: Sorry, no can do (also I typed up most of my thoughts before I saw your post).

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Mar 15, 2013 6:52 pm I don't think we have any evidence to conclude that this sensei-guy is a horrible person. We have no indications that any neglect etc took place, and hey, we hear that they guy even encouraged Shinji to take up to Cello, so he seems to have tried to give the kid some imput. It's probably more that a trained professional/teacher, no matter how dedicated, can't replace an actual parent/family member - for example, it would have been highly improper for some "teacher" to give Shinji a hug once in a while. I mean, he never appears in any flashbacks meant to explain the way Shinji is - occassional mentions seem to imply that Shinji trusts what the guy says (and he did seem to have given Shinji straight answers) and regards his place as fairly safe, even if there wasn't much of a personal connection (After all, ep 2 implies that he's not used to chatting at the table) Presumably, Shinji got it into his head that he's unwanted on this earth by the time Gendo dumped him, and tried to bother whoever "burdened" themselves with him as little as possible, resulting in the two generally getting along, but never really bonding on a personal level. That's also why the sensei guy doesn't call - His job is over. Even in a boarding school where the kid live there, few teachers will bother to check up on what the kids do once they leave the school for years after. Gendo probably meant well, thinking that someone whose actual job it is to look after children would be a good choice for someone to leave Shinji with (Here, we also see the whole rational/emotional disconnect between the Ikaris - Shinji would probably have prefered adoptive parents or something. But hey, look at that - Gendo did NOT just dump him in an orphanage - and as far as we hear, the contact between them, sparse as it may have been, did not completely stop untill some nebulous incident three years prior, involving the grave and perhaps Shinji comfronting Gendo about Yui's death. ) Sure, Gendo's not winning any parent-of-the-year awards anytime soon, but it's lack of competence (and confidence more than anything - Look at Rei. They seem to have a silent uderstanding/unspoken trust between them, and get along nicely. Rei's not the most happy person in the world, but that's more related to aspects that wouldn't be there with Shinji, like the whole replacability business - Then again, Gendo seems to have completely failed to notice that Rei was unhappy with that. He did NOT see number three's betrayal comming in the slightest... Yeah, he wasn't a particularly good father to Rei, but he was something of a father there, enough to say that his humble parenting skills may have been preferable to no parents at all.../ that Shinji may have benefited from Gendo at least trying to raise him - on the other hand, there's an implication that he did and that it did not go all too well, he's about 3 when Yui goes splat, but the script of ep 01 discribes him as "4 or 5" in that one eternally repeated pic of him bawling at the train station...), combined with a relevant difference in one aspect of their personalities, not lack of care/interest/goodwill. There's a marked difference between how he treats Shinji and Rei, and how he treats people he actually doersn't give a damn about, like poor Ritsuko. He destroys her with one little sentence, one complete exertion of power - I have little doubt that he could similarly manipulate Shinji with fairly little words, given just how starved the kid is for any positive recognition, but Gendo never went and abused that, not even when Shinji threatened to leave in ep 4, which would've left Gendo without usable pilots at his disposal. Another thing is, of course, that Gendo has some disconnect there, which you might call psychopathic - wether he likes a person and wether he's ready to sacrifice them for the plan are utterly unrelated to him. He sure doesn't like sending a half-dead Rei out in unit 01, he tried all other alternatives and avoids it whenever possible, but if there's no other possibility... he does it, without regrets, because, "there is no other way."... That's why I don't like it when spinoffs of FF writers swap the teacher for some random relative (appart from the indication that Yui's family, according to CI, is SEELE-affilated . The last thing Gendo needs is them indoctrinating his potential crucial pilot candidate.), it takes away from the fact that Shinji's supposed to be the result of getting little human warmth at all, not too different from Rei, even if her case is more extreme (then again, those manga relatives were implied to basically treat him like Arima from Kare Kano got treated, minus the kind adoptive parents part, the "black sheeps kid thing"...)

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Sat Mar 16, 2013 3:46 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The emergency would have happened while Shinji was on the train; before then everything would be post-2I normal.
